items.title: Is shipping waste killing fish in Chukai? DOE investigates major oil slick
items.summary: CHUKAI, April 15 — The Teluk Kalong beach here has been polluted with oil slicks believed to be remnants of oil from ships anchored not far from the beach since Monday. He said he also found dozens of dead fish and squid floating in the sea near the beach. Another fisherman Mohamad Hasnam Khalid, 55, said that the stain, believed to be oil residue, had never occurred on the beach before.

items.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 stranded Iranian sailors
items.summary: Deputy Defence Minister Aruna Jayasekara said 32 sailors rescued from the IRIS Dena -- a frigate attacked on March 4 just off Sri Lanka -- and another 206 from the IRIS Bushehr left on Tuesday. Official sources said 15 Iranian sailors will remain in Sri Lanka to operate the IRIS Bushehr, which is anchored off Trincomalee in the northeast of the island. Last month, Iran's ambassador to Sri Lanka Alireza Delkhosh said Tehran was in talks with Colombo to repatriate sailors from the IRIS Bushehr which was given safe harbour in Sri Lanka after the IRIS Dena was sunk.

items.title: Umbrella alert: MetMalaysia forecasts a very wet afternoon for seven states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for parts of seven states in Peninsular Malaysia and several divisions in Sarawak, effective until 4:00 pm today. In an alert issued at 12:30 pm, the agency advised residents in the affected areas to prepare for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds. In Peninsular Malaysia, the warning covers Langkawi in Kedah; the districts of Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim in Perak; and Hulu Selangor.

items.title: No slowdown at counters as govt rolls out WFH for civil servants
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Government counter services across the capital continued to operate smoothly today, despite the rollout of a work-from-home (WFH) arrangement involving some 200,000 civil servants. Members of the public said transactions were completed within the usual timeframe. Badrul Azfarawi Faizaluddin, 21, from Selayang, said services at UTC Keramat were operating normally.

items.title: Contractor tells court work halted after mystery bags found in Rosmah-linked unit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A renovation contractor told the High Court he stumbled upon several large bags inside a Pavilion Residences unit a day before it was raided by police in investigations linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd scandal. Chong Tong Leong, 53, said he immediately stopped renovation works and left the premises over safety concerns. "Upon entering the unit, I saw several large bags (luggage) in a downstairs room.

items.title: IOI Properties accelerates capital recycling as Malaysia reit IPO set for FY27
items.summary: Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB) said while the reit exercise marks a key step in crystallising asset value, it represents only the beginning of the group's wider monetisation pipeline. "Once the Malaysian REIT is listed, focus will shift towards the monetisation of its Singapore assets, which serve as the next re-rating catalyst," HLIB said in a note. In FY25, IOI Properties raised its dividend per share (DPS) to 8.0 sen from 5.0 sen previously, surprising investors who had anticipated higher gearing levels following the South Beach acquisition.

items.title: Uber commits US$10bil to robotaxis in strategy shift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Uber has committed more than US$10 billion to buying thousands of autonomous vehicles and taking stakes in their developers, breaking from its asset-light "gig economy" business model to avoid disruption from robotaxis, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday. Uber did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment. These deals put Uber on track to invest more than US$2.5 billion in equity stakes and spend over US$7.5 billion on robotaxi fleets in the next few years, FT reported citing their calculations based on analyst estimates and people familiar with Uber's deals.

items.title: Policy shifts drive strong momentum in Malaysia's solar sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's solar sector is gaining strong momentum driven by a series of supportive policy shifts that are reshaping the country's renewable energy landscape and accelerating both adoption and investment. "These policy dynamics are rapidly unlocking the potential of Malaysia's rooftop solar and solar-plus-storage market, supporting the country's goal of achieving 70 per cent renewable energy by 2050," he said during Malaysia's first Brand Launch & Partnership conference, held here, recently. Wang reaffirmed Skyworth's long-term commitment to the new energy industry, stressing the importance of localisation in driving sustainable low-carbon growth across global markets.

items.title: 5E Resources eyes growth from B15 biodiesel push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Waste management services provider 5E Resources Holdings Bhd is poised to leverage the government's move to raise the biodiesel blend rate to B15, expecting increased waste generation from the biodiesel industry to support its growth. Chief executive officer Lim Te Hua said the group's expansion in Johor Bahru, slated to commence operations in the second half of 2026, would enable it to handle a larger share of waste from customers. He said the group currently manages only 20 per cent of waste from one biodiesel industry customer in Tanjung Langsat, Johor Bahru, due to quota constraints.

items.title: From teacher to lawyer, San Markan fulfils lifelong ambition
items.summary: "He always wanted to become a lawyer," said long-time friend KP Sudesh, who has known San Markan for more than four decades. Yesterday, family members and friends bade farewell to San Markan. That ambition eventually took San Markan far from home.

items.title: Manforce eyes RM30.4mil from ACE Market IPO, listing set for May 6
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Manforce Group Bhd today launched its prospectus in conjunction with its upcoming initial public offering (IPO) on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia, which is expected to raise RM30.4 million in gross proceeds, with its listing slated for May 6, 2026. The IPO comprises a public issue of 79.99 million new ordinary shares, representing 20 per cent of the group's enlarged issued share capital of 399.98 million shares, and an offer for sale of 19.99 million existing shares, representing five per cent of the enlarged issued share capital. The IPO shares will be issued at 38 sen per share.

items.title: Rosmah’s US$14.6m jewellery lawsuit: High Court to rule on June 10
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The High Court today fixed June 10 for its decision on the US$14.6 million (approximately RM69 million) lawsuit filed by Lebanese jeweller Global Royalty Trading SAL against Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor over 43 pieces of missing jewellery. In her defence, Rosmah has maintained that the original consignment of 44 pieces of jewellery was seized by police during a raid on a Pavilion Residences unit in May 2018, shortly after the 14th General Election. Also testifying was contractor Chong Tong Leong, who told the court he was renovating the Pavilion Residences unit between March and May 2018.

items.title: Johor water supply set for 23-hour disruption in JB, Kota Tinggi and Pontian
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The districts of Johor Baru, Kota Tinggi, and Pontian will experience a planned water supply disruption for 23 hours on April 21 and 22. Johor water operator Ranhill SAJ Sdn Bhd announced that the disruption for maintenance work at the Sungai Johor water treatment plant is scheduled to begin at 9am Tuesday and will run until 8am Wednesday. "Water storage tanks at premises can last up to 24 hours under normal use.

items.title: Petronas extends fuel supply assurance to June amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) says fuel supply across its nationwide network of stations is secured through the end of June 2026, extending an earlier projection of end-May coverage. "Petronas supplies about 50 per cent of the country's fuel needs through its public-listed subsidiary, Petronas Dagangan Bhd. Petronas reaffirmed its commitment to ensuring a stable and reliable fuel supply nationwide, while urging the public to act responsibly.

items.title: Man killed, wife injured in Linkedua crash during routine commute to Singapore
items.summary: Iskandar Puteri police chief M Kumarasan said the incident, which occurred at about 2.40pm, was believed to have happened when the motorcycle ridden by the 31-year-old victim, with his 29-year-old wife as pillion, lost control and struck the rear of a lorry. “The motorcyclist, a local man, was pronounced dead at the scene due to head injuries. “The wife also sustained injuries to her head and left arm and was taken to Hospital Sultanah Aminah for treatment.

items.title: Puchong raid nets four tonnes of illegal rubber gloves, says LGM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The Malaysian Rubber Board (LGM) seized about four tonnes of illegal rubber gloves stored in sacks, along with a weighing scale, in a raid on a property in Puchong, Selangor, yesterday. In a statement, LGM said the premises were believed to be actively involved in the buying, storing and selling of rejected and scrap rubber gloves without a permit. LGM said such activities constitute an offence under Regulation 22(1)(b) of the Malaysian Rubber Board (Licensing and Permit) Regulations 2014.

items.title: Perak to review need for supplementary budget amid West Asia tensions
items.summary: IPOH, April 15 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the state government will study whether there is a need to table a supplementary budget following concerns over the escalating West Asia conflict. He was responding to an additional question from Kepayang assemblyman Nga Kor Ming, who asked whether the state government is prepared to table a supplementary budget in light of the global crisis. Saarani said the state government has extensive experience in managing past global crises.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed, wife hurt in Johor expressway crash
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: A 31-year-old man was killed and his wife injured after their motorcycle crashed into the back of a lorry at Km0.6 of the Second Link Expressway southbound yesterday. Iskandar Puteri police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the motorcyclist is believed to have lost control before colliding with the rear of the lorry, which was moving slowly due to traffic congestion. The lorry driver was unhurt.

items.title: Sabah dismemberment murder: Accused sent for 30-day mental observation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A septuagenarian charged with murdering a woman whose dismembered body parts were found in Indah Permai, Sepanggar, has been ordered to undergo 30 days of observation at Hospital Mesra. Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus allowed an application by the defence to refer Piluta Samad, 71, for psychiatric evaluation under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code. Piluta was charged on February 27 with the murder of Hamidah Husin, also known as Lisa, 44.

items.title: Schools cannot withhold certificates over PTA fees, says N. Sembilan Education Dept
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 15 — The Negeri Sembilan Education Department (JPNS) has stressed that schools are not allowed to withhold students’ certificates or official documents due to failure to contribute to the Parent-Teacher Association (PTA). In a statement today, JPNS said PTA contributions are voluntary and must not be made a condition in any matters involving students. “JPNS has taken note of content circulating on the TikTok platform regarding allegations of a student’s certificate being withheld.

items.title: South Korea jails US streamer Johnny Somali for statue outrage
items.summary: SEOUL: An American YouTuber who caused outrage for filming himself kissing a statue commemorating Korean wartime sex slaves has been sentenced to six months in prison, a court in Seoul said on Wednesday. South Korean authorities indicted Somali – whose real name is Ramsey Khalid Ismael – in 2024 on public order violations and obstruction of business, and banned him from leaving the country. "The court has sentenced him to six months in prison," a representative of the Seoul Western District Court told AFP when asked about Somali's case on Wednesday.

items.title: Dry taps alert: JB, Kota Tinggi, Pontian to face 23-hour water cut on April 21 and 22
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 15 — Three districts, namely Johor Bahru, Kota Tinggi and Pontian, are scheduled to experience a planned water supply disruption for 23 hours on April 21 and 22. Johor water operator, Ranhill SAJ Sdn Bhd, announced that the disruption is scheduled to begin at 9am Tuesday to 8am Wednesday for maintenance work at Sungai Johor Water Treatment Plant. Consumers are also advised to obtain information regarding water supply disruptions through Ranhill SAJ’s official complaint channel at 1800887474, or email customer.care@ranhillsaj.com.my or SMS: ATRanhillSAJ Complaint to number 63001.

items.title: Perak faces medical officer shortfall as doctor-to-population ratio lags national level
items.summary: Perak Human Resources, Health, Indian Community Affairs and National Integration Committee chairman Datuk A. Sivanesan said according to the Health Facts 2025 publication by the Ministry of Health (MoH), the ratio in Perak currently stands at 1 doctor for every 466 people compared to the national average of 1 doctor for every 403 people. He said there is a need to increase the number of medical officers across all hospitals, particularly specialist hospitals, to cope with rising demand. He said that MoH is aware of the optimal number of medical officers needed in the state, based on human resource requirements and monthly staffing reports submitted by the Perak State Health Department (JKN Perak).

items.title: Bank Islam offers assistance to customers amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Islam Malaysia Bhd has launched its Prihatin Programme to provide targeted assistance to customers affected by the ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties. The initiative is designed to ease immediate financial pressures, support cash flow continuity, and strengthen customer resilience during a period of heightened global volatility. The bank said assistance will be tailored to meet the specific needs of both individual and business customers, taking into account their respective financial situations.

items.title: Prince Harry speaks of struggles of fatherhood on Australia tour
items.summary: SYDNEY: Britain's Prince Harry spoke on Wednesday of the struggles of early fatherhood, as he visited a Melbourne football club on the second day of his tour of Australia with wife Meghan, Duchess of Sussex. The couple is on a four-day trip to the country, with engagements covering sport, mental health and veterans' affairs. They last visited Australia in 2018 while still working royals, announcing Meghan's first pregnancy hours after arriving in Sydney.

items.title: Runaway suspect in viral parang attack remanded for seven days
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A 47-year-old man who had been on the run since Sunday for allegedly causing grievous hurt to two men in Jalan Besar, Telok Ayer Tawar, has been remanded for seven days to assist in investigations. The remand order, effective today, was granted by magistrate Balqis Roslin at the Butterworth court. He was arrested at Penang Sentral about 4pm yesterday after evading authorities for two days.

items.title: MCMC probes individual over false diesel export claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating an individual suspected of spreading false news alleging that Malaysia supplied 329,000 barrels of diesel to the Philippines. The MCMC listed several other false claims, including exaggerated fuel price hikes, false electricity tariff increases, and allegations that Malaysian vessels were paying tolls to Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. It also cited false claims that Budi95 subsidies were being extended to Singaporeans.

items.title: Sarawak premier tells Bintulu Port to shape future of how trade is conducted
items.summary: Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the port’s role must evolve beyond facilitating trade to shaping how trade is conducted in the future. “Today, Bintulu Port stands as the pulse of Sarawak’s economy, supporting industries, enabling trade, and connecting Sarawak to the world,” he said during Bintulu Port Holdings Berhad’s (BPHB) 30th anniversary dinner here on Tuesday. As Sarawak advances under the Post Covid-19 Development Strategy 2030 (PCDS 2030), he said Bintulu Port’s importance will continue to grow, requiring not only expansion in capacity but also enhancement in capability.

items.title: No brakes: RTD seizes 2 heavily modified motorcycles in Melaka operation
items.summary: Melaka RTD director Siti Zarina Mohd Yusop said the group, believed to be heading towards Klebang, made dangerous U-turns in an attempt to escape. Investigations also found the crashed motorcycle had no valid road tax and insurance. A second motorcycle was also seized for multiple serious technical offences.

items.title: Proposed Samalaju-Kidurong-Bintulu rail link system undergoing feasibility study, says Sarawak premier
items.summary: KUCHING, April 15 — A series of new infrastructural and technological developments are set to transform Bintulu Port into a modern, integrated, and future-ready maritime hub, said the Premier. Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said among the key initiatives is a proposed rail link system connecting Samalaju Port and Bintulu Port, with a feasibility study currently underway. “While Bintulu Port remains the flagship, our vision extends to the entire Sarawak port ecosystem.

items.title: RM800mil federal govt projects boost Penang water supply resilience
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The federal government is implementing water supply projects worth more than RM800 million in Penang to boost resilience and long-term security. Penang Water Supply Corporation chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through its Water Supply Division, is undertaking five projects with a total estimated cost of RM837.5 million. Other works include RM26.5 million for water intake upgrades in Teluk Bahang, scheduled for completion in 2029, and a RM3 million statewide raw water study.

items.title: Perak to study need for supplementary budget amid global economic pressures
items.summary: IPOH: Perak will study the need for a supplementary budget as it assesses global economic pressures, with a review underway before any decision is made. "Based on our experience during Covid-19, we did not table a supplementary budget but realigned the existing budget," he said. The Perak State Legislative Assembly last December approved the state's RM1.61 billion Budget for 2026, the largest in its history.

items.title: Speaker awaits official word on Hamzah's reported resignation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul says he has yet to receive any official notification regarding the resignation of Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as Opposition Leader. "Not yet (received the letter)," he said. On Sunday, Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said Hamzah was no longer holding the post.

items.title: Foreign investments in Perak stable despite global crisis - Saarani
items.summary: IPOH: Foreign investment in Perak remains resilient despite ongoing global economic uncertainty, with no major cancellations recorded and existing projects continuing as planned. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said continuous monitoring showed that the impact on the state's investment landscape remained minimal and under control. "Based on current monitoring, the foreign investment landscape in Malaysia in general and in Perak in particular remains resilient.

items.title: Penang gets RM800m federal boost for water security projects
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 15 — The Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation will implement five key water supply projects in Penang worth an estimated RM800 million to bolster the state’s water security, the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) announced today. According to PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan, the federally-funded projects are designed to boost the functionality of the Expanded Mengkuang Dam (EMD), enable more efficient water abstraction from Sungai Muda, and upgrade services across the state. “These projects will effectively upgrade water supply services and enable faster refilling of the Teluk Bahang Dam,” Pathmanathan said in a statement.

items.title: Rising diesel costs back Sarawak’s energy shift, says Abang Johari
items.summary: KUCHING, April 15 — Sarawak’s early push into alternative energy has placed the state on the right track, especially as global diesel prices continue to surge amid geopolitical tensions, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. Abang Johari said Sarawak is no longer merely exploring alternative energy but is already positioning itself as a player in renewable and green energy. They are about energy security from multiple sources of energy,” he said.

items.title: Aviation, logistics jobs at risk if Middle East conflict persists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The logistics sector, particularly aviation, is among the first to be affected in terms of employment and operations if the Middle East conflict persists. IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said aviation is the most exposed, as fuel is its largest cost component. Mohd Sedek said employment risks tend to build gradually, particularly in sectors most sensitive to energy costs and global demand.

items.title: No official letter yet on Hamzah Zainudin’s resignation as Opposition Leader, says Speaker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has yet to receive any notification letter on the resignation of Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as Opposition Leader. “Not yet (received the letter),” he said briefly. Last Sunday, Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar confirmed that Hamzah is no longer holding the post of Opposition Leader.

items.title: Trump says Iran war is 'close to over' [WATCH]
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump, in an interview on Wednesday, said the US-Israeli war on Iran is "close to over", Anadolu Ajansi reported. I mean, I view it as very close to over," Trump told Fox Business anchor Maria Bartiromo when asked if the war is "over." — BERNAMA PRESIDENT TRUMP: WAR WITH IRAN IS "CLOSE TO OVER."

items.title: [UPDATED] Bentong camp assault: Police record 16 statements, more witnesses may be called
items.summary: KUANTAN: Police have recorded statements from 16 individuals in connection with the alleged assault of Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, who died on April 11 after 12 days in critical condition. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said authorities may recall some individuals or summon additional witnesses after the case was reclassified as murder. "The suspect was initially charged with assault on April 6 in Bentong, and the case was later reclassified as murder after the victim's death.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM APRIL 15, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Wednesday, in line with gains across regional markets, as optimism grew over a possible easing of tensions in Middle East. The FBM KLCI climbed to nearly the 1,690 mark yesterday, supported by improved regional sentiment and selective bargain hunting. In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM294,757.

items.title: [UPDATED] Traffic under control on first day of WFH implementation for public sector
items.summary: He said that, as of 9.20am, slow-moving traffic was reported on several stretches of the North-South Expressway, including Sungai Buloh Hospital to Bukit Lanjan, Putra Mahkota to Kajang, and from the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza towards the city centre. "Traffic flow was also reported to be slow along the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) from Damansara to Damansara Utama, as well as on the Shah Alam Expressway (KESAS) between Bukit Rimau and Persiaran Kewajipan, SUKOM and Awan Kecil, Awan Besar and Kinrara, Sunway and Persiaran Kewajipan, and Bandar Botanik and Pandamaran. "Slow traffic was also observed on the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite) along several stretches, namely Putrajaya to Putra Heights, USJ to Shah Alam, Putra Heights to Bandar Saujana Putra, the Putrajaya-Cyberjaya Toll Plaza, as well as on the Grand Saga Highway (E7) from Bandar Tun Hussein Onn to the city centre," he said.

items.title: Gold steadies near one-week high, eyes on US–Iran talks
items.summary: FUNDAMENTALS Spot gold held steady at US$4,841.76 per ounce as of 0110 GMT, after hitting its highest level since April 8 earlier in the session. US gold futures for June delivery rose 0.3 per cent to US$4,866.50. Among other metals, spot silver rose 0.4 per cent to US$79.87 per ounce, platinum gained 1.1 per cent to US$2,127.45, and palladium was up 0.1 per cent to US$1,596.28.

items.title: JD Vance: US-Iran mistrust cannot be solved overnight
items.summary: Talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted Washington to impose a blockade on Iranian ports. Iranian negotiators wanted to make a deal, he said. The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Magnitude 5.1 earthquake strikes Bali Sea, no tsunami threat to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — A moderate earthquake with a magnitude of 5.1 struck the Bali Sea at 10:10 am today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has reported. The epicentre of the quake was located 111 kilometres north of Mataram, Indonesia, at a depth of 474 kilometres. MetMalaysia confirmed that the earthquake poses no tsunami threat to Malaysia.

items.title: Klang Valley traffic stays normal as govt work-from-home policy begins
items.summary: He said that, as of 9.20am, slow-moving traffic was reported on several stretches of the North-South Expressway, including Sungai Buloh Hospital to Bukit Lanjan, Putra Mahkota to Kajang, and from the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza towards the city centre. “Traffic flow was also reported to be slow along the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) from Damansara to Damansara Utama, as well as on the Shah Alam Expressway (KESAS) between Bukit Rimau and Persiaran Kewajipan, SUKOM and Awan Kecil, Awan Besar and Kinrara, Sunway and Persiaran Kewajipan, and Bandar Botanik and Pandamaran. “Slow traffic was also observed on the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite) along several stretches, namely Putrajaya to Putra Heights, USJ to Shah Alam, Putra Heights to Bandar Saujana Putra, the Putrajaya-Cyberjaya Toll Plaza, as well as on the Grand Saga Highway (E7) from Bandar Tun Hussein Onn to the city centre,” he said.

items.title: MCMC questions individual over fake news on diesel shipment to Philippines
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 15 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating an individual suspected of spreading false news that Malaysia supplied 329,000 barrels of diesel to the Philippines, when the fuel belongs to a private trading company. In a statement today, the MCMC confirmed it has recorded a statement from the suspect and seized a communication device believed to have been used to upload the false content. “As of 14 April, a total of 47 investigation papers have been opened involving the spread of false news regarding the global energy crisis on social media and digital media platforms, following the conflict in West Asia.

items.title: GOF busts RM700,000 drug haul in Pasir Mas
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 15 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Tenggara Brigade seized more than 200 kilogrammes (kg) of suspected cannabis worth RM709,450 in a patrol operation around Jeram Perdah, Pasir Mas, on Monday. Tenggara Brigade GOF commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was made at 8.30 pm under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan after personnel stopped a suspicious vehicle in Kampung Kubang Puyu Meranti, Pasir Mas. He said a team conducting motorcycle patrols in Kampung Siram Pohon Tanjung spotted a white Proton Saga BLM in suspicious circumstances before attempting to stop it.

items.title: JPJ crackdown nets 26 bikes, 283 summonses in DUKE illegal racing ops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department (JPJ) seized 26 motorcycles in a special operation against illegal racing on the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) last Sunday. Kuala Lumpur JPJ director Hamidi Adam said in a statement today that 283 summonses were issued and 80 individuals were checked during the two-hour operation by the department’s Falcon Unit, which started at 3am. He said serious offences detected under the Road Transport Act 1987 included missing side mirrors, fancy or cloned registration plates, modified exhausts causing excessive noise, lack of rear brakes, and missing engine or chassis numbers.

items.title: Aaron, Gobind extend Vishu greetings, call for stronger neighbourly spirit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang and Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo today extended Vishu greetings to the Malayalee community in Malaysia. Aaron said the Vishu celebration marks the start of the new year in the Malayalam calendar, while also symbolising hope, well-being and better beginnings. “In the cultural diversity we possess, every celebration such as Vishu becomes a bridge that connects us as one big Malaysian family,” said Aaron.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for Langkawi, parts of Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for Langkawi, Kedah, and parts of Johor, effective until noon today. In an alert issued at 9am, the national weather agency specified that the affected areas in Johor are Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru. Residents in these locations are advised to prepare for thunderstorms, heavy rainfall, and strong winds.

items.title: Bus driver in viral lap video to be questioned by police today
items.summary: JASIN, April 15 — Police have confirmed that the male express bus driver and a woman believed to be known to him will appear at the Jasin District Police Headquarters tomorrow to assist in investigations into a viral video showing him driving the bus while holding the woman on his lap. “The woman is in Kedah and will head to Kuala Lumpur tonight, and both of them will come to the Jasin District Police Headquarters tomorrow for questioning,” he said in a statement here yesterday. An eight‑second Facebook video went viral, showing the woman sitting on the driver’s lap as the vehicle moved.

items.title: Ampang cops hunt driver in Taman Muda hit-and-run that left pedestrian seriously injured
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — Police are looking for the driver who injured a pedestrian in a hit-and-run incident along Jalan Bunga Mawar, Taman Muda here at 8.19pm on Monday. Ampang Jaya district police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said they received a report on the hit-and-run at 12.39 am, adding that preliminary investigations revealed that the victim was crossing from left to right of the road near the pedestrian crossing when they were hit. Khairul Anuar urged the driver involved to come forward and lodge a police report at the Ampang Jaya district police headquarters Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division and to fully cooperate with investigations.

items.title: Prisons Dept denies SOP breach in escort convoy, cites technical glitch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The Prisons Department stressed that there were no violations of standard operating procedures in the inmate escort operation to court yesterday, and every step taken by officers were based on security considerations, operations priority and current situation on the field. The escort operation involved the movement of a convoy from Kajang Prison to several court locations as well as hospitals around the Klang Valley, consisting of two buses and a van as the lead vehicle. “The convoy movement also involved a planned route separation, where the lead vehicle (van) continued its journey to the hospital, while the escort buses proceeded to the designated courts.

items.title: DBKL intensifies crackdown on KLCC and Saloma Link photo touts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — Authorities have taken action against unauthorised photographers operating around the KLCC and Saloma Link areas, seizing equipment and issuing multiple compounds and court notices in a joint operation on Monday. The multi-agency operation, dubbed “Op Lens,” was carried out by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) in collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department, and the National Registration Department. In a statement, DBKL confirmed it issued four compound notices for operating without a licence, seized four sets of photography equipment, and served two notices for individuals to appear in court.

items.title: Court of Appeal rules ex-AirAsia co-pilot was unlawfully dismissed, awards RM147,400
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 15 — The Court of Appeal yesterday awarded RM147,400 in compensation and back wages to a former AirAsia Berhad (AAB) co-pilot who was terminated during the Covid-19 pandemic in 2020. The court, however, deducted RM16,500 previously paid by AAB as retrenchment benefits and RM23,100 from his post-dismissal earnings. Delivering the court’s unanimous decision, Justice Azmi said the Industrial Court and High Court erred in relying on AirAsia Group Berhad’s (AAGB) consolidated losses to justify retrenching an employee of AAB, which is a separate legal entity.

items.title: Anwar urges Bank Rakyat to reinvent, stay competitive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — Bank Kerjasama Rakyat Malaysia Bhd (Bank Rakyat) needs to explore new opportunities towards creating a more competitive and sustainable business model for mutual benefit. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Bank Rakyat also needs to carry out its founding mandate with more courage and vision. Anwar received a courtesy call from Bank Rakyat chairman Datuk Mohd Irwan Mohd Mubarak and Bank Rakyat chief executive officer Ahmad Shahril Mohd Shariff and was briefed on the bank’s financial performance in 2025 and its direction in the coming year.

items.title: Indonesian man arrested after newborn found buried in Labu
items.summary: NILAI, April 15 — Police have arrested an Indonesian man after a newborn baby boy was found buried at a house in the Labu area here. Nilai District Police Chief Supt Johari Yahya confirmed that a police report was lodged at 9:53am yesterday by a medical officer from Tuanku Jaafar Hospital Seremban. Acting on the information, a police team, including the D10 Forensic Division from the Negeri Sembilan Contingent, investigated the scene and exhumed the infant’s body.

items.title: Anwar: NEAC acts to secure power supply and basic goods, cushion rakyat from energy squeeze
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 15 — The National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting has set a clear direction in managing the global energy crisis, which is increasingly acute and expected to be prolonged, by emphasising the main approach to guaranteeing the supply of energy and basic goods so that the people’s daily lives are not affected. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said efforts to manage the energy crisis were driven by the important role of Petroliam Nasional Bhd and early planning to ensure that supplies remain sufficient in the coming months. Anwar said demand management has also been improved through efficient energy use, including the implementation of Work From Home for civil servants starting tomorrow.

items.title: IMF lifts Malaysia’s 2026 growth outlook to 4.7pc, 4.3pc in 2027
items.summary: According to its April 2026 World Economic Outlook (WEO) released yesterday, the IMF also estimated Malaysia’s real GDP growth at 4.3 per cent in 2027. In 2025, Malaysia recorded a strong growth of 5.2 per cent, supported by resilient domestic demand. IMF said growth in advanced economies is projected to be 1.8 per cent in 2026 and 1.7 per cent in 2027.
